title: Scope: Local vs Global Variables
description: Learn where variables exist and how functions see them.

slides.title: Variable Scope 🌐
slides.content: Variables defined inside a function are **local** to that function – they don't exist outside. Variables defined outside are **global** and can be accessed inside, but not modified unless explicitly assigned.

slides.title: Local Variables

quizData.explanation: Inside f, local x is assigned 10, returned. Global x remains 5.

slides.title: Modifying Global Variables
slides.content:
To modify a global variable inside a function, use the super-assignment operator `<<-` (but use sparingly – it can lead to confusing code).

```r
y <- 1
f <- function() {
  y <<- 2
}
```

slides.title: Super Assignment
slides.codeSnippet:
counter <- 0
increment <- function() {
  counter <<- counter + 1
}
increment()
increment()
print(counter)

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Avoid modifying global variables inside functions. Instead, return values and assign them outside. This makes code more predictable.
